title: juglans jamaicensis subsp. insularis
intro: Juglans jamaicensis subsp. insularis, also known as Jamaican walnut, is a subspecies of the Jamaican walnut tree, a deciduous tree native to Jamaica. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties. The health benefits of Jamaican walnut include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve cardiovascular health, and support immune function. Jamaican walnut can be prepared as a herbal tea, tincture, or infused oil, and is also used in traditional remedies for skin conditions and digestive issues.
